sections.heading: PREAMBLE
sections.body:
In the aftermath of the Omicron Collapse, it became clear that existing protocols were insufficient to manage the scale and nature of the incursion. The Threshold Accords represent a binding operational framework intended to standardize response, classify all relevant intelligence, and ensure the continuity of productive operations within affected sectors.

All personnel assigned to Sector 9 are required to acknowledge receipt and comprehension of this document prior to their first operational cycle. Failure to sign constitutes grounds for immediate reassignment.

sections.heading: ARTICLE 1 — CLASSIFICATION
sections.body:
All intelligence pertaining to void incursions, entity contacts, and personnel affected by void exposure is classified Omega-level by default. Unauthorized disclosure to non-Sector personnel constitutes a breach of operational security and will be addressed accordingly.

Personnel are reminded that 'addressing breaches accordingly' is not a euphemism. Reference: Operative Counselor Resolution 14-B.

sections.heading: ARTICLE 4 — PRODUCTIVITY METRICS
sections.body:
Sector 9 operatives are evaluated on a continuous performance cycle. Metrics are compiled from:

— Mission completion rate
— Equipment return rate
— Cognitive coherence scores (administered bi-weekly)
— Incident report submission timeliness
— Peer assessment scores

Operatives falling below the 85th percentile on any single metric for two consecutive cycles will be placed on Supervised Status. Three consecutive cycles below threshold triggers automatic reassignment review.

Note: Supervised Status does not affect your base compensation. It does affect your priority score for equipment requisition and shift selection.

sections.heading: ARTICLE 7 — VOID-EXPOSED PERSONNEL
sections.body:
Any operative confirmed to have experienced direct void exposure is subject to mandatory cognitive monitoring for a minimum of 12 operational cycles. During this period:

— The operative may not serve in leadership capacity
— The operative's incident reports require co-signature from a non-exposed supervisor
— The operative's stated perceptions during the monitoring period are classified as Unreliable Testimony and cannot form the basis of formal complaints

This article exists to protect the operative and the integrity of Sector 9's data. It is not punitive.

sections.heading: ARTICLE 9 — EQUIPMENT
sections.body:
All void-derived materials and equipment remain property of Sector 9 regardless of operational context. Operatives do not own their gear. Gear is assigned. Gear may be reassigned at any time.

Loss of gear in the field is documented. Repeated loss triggers a review under Article 4.

sections.heading: ARTICLE 12 — THE CLOSING PROVISION
sections.body:
In situations where a sector or facility cannot be contained, Sector 9 Command retains the authority to classify it as a Total Loss Zone and cease all recovery operations. Personnel within a declared Total Loss Zone at time of classification are presumed non-recoverable and are administratively closed.

This provision has been exercised once. Cycle 4417. We do not discuss the specifics publicly. Those who were there understand.

sections.heading: ACKNOWLEDGMENT
sections.body:
By continuing your assignment, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to operate within the Threshold Accords. You acknowledge that Sector 9 exists to serve a necessary function, that the work is hard, and that not everyone makes it.

Welcome aboard. We're glad you're here.
